keyAdditions: Product-style README front door that points to the single canonical USAGE.md prompt
Create-once, integrity-checked initial rebuild baseline plus separate current-state reporting, so later site changes do not revoke the first completed milestone
Post-baseline repair and extension lifecycle with status, begin, complete, abandon, impact-targeted evidence, and optional later full-verification checkpoints

Verifier-owned state-bound global chrome regression with direct CDP desktop/mobile captures, verified-anchor dynamic masks, automatic PageRegion/theme/block/menu/navigation impact detection, and fail-closed brand/header/nav/footer/link/mobile/layout comparisons
Verifier-owned axe-core 4.10.3 execution on every primary-route desktop/mobile capture, with pinned licensed source, raw state-bound results, and fail-closed WCAG 2.2 A/AA coverage
Exact state binding across tracked config, runtime facts, declared editorial revisions, managed public assets, stable routes, and recursively manifested packet evidence

Optional verifier-owned disposable DDEV reproduction from an independent exact-HEAD clone, typed digest-bound adapters, portable config/entity/file/route comparison, working-target before/after proof, and explicit clean-install versus snapshot modes

Launch-only verifier-owned assembly rerun proof from an exact-HEAD pre-assembly substrate: typed dry-run reconciliation, provenance-bounded deletes with per-prefix dual opt-in, verifier-selected interrupted prefixes, verifier-owned database/file restoration, durable persistence and opaque entity-identity guards, exact no-op reruns, live-applicable extension fixtures, aggregate budgets, and zero working-target Drupal commands
Project-local verification observability with overlap-aware phase timing, matched workload fingerprints, and compact next-action state kept outside completion evidence

Opt-in remote-browser Global Chrome shadow prediction with exact persisted report/run binding, seed plus two-confirmation qualification, permanent mismatch quarantine, and no verification skipping or evidence authority
docs/site-lifecycle.md guidance for continuing from a verified Drupal foundation without mandating full source review, Git commits, Canvas, or launch gates for every change
Self-contained installable Agent Skill with project-local runtime assets, target initializer, and verifiers
Marker-managed AGENTS.md integration that preserves One Line Installer, Drupal AI best-practices, and user-authored instructions
NPM packaging exclusions for local agent settings, lock state, and generated archives
Node 20 continuous integration for tests, installed-skill synchronization, and package dry-run checks
Pinned DDEV Selenium/Chromium sidecar with host-side setup, fail-closed CDP and axe preflight, service-only repair, and a non-skipping real-browser CI smoke
Private vulnerability reporting policy for security-sensitive findings
Newcomer-facing README and START.md that position the kit for Drupal-curious developers, designers, agencies, and site builders
Positioning page reframed around trying Drupal CMS with an AI coding agent and keeping the result reviewable
README quickstart with a recommended source-site prompt and a separate brief-only prompt, each requiring exactly one build input
START.md one-screen onboarding with prerequisites, workspace topology, two clearly separated input prompts, four moves, and stake-my-name review bar

Canonical stake-my-name verdict, with long architecture checklist demoted to supporting evidence
Recipe discovery commands for Composer package evidence, installed recipe paths, recipe info, and recipe application

Recipe default-owner rule: before custom content types, Views, workflows, or cross-cutting features, discover matching maintained Drupal CMS recipes and record applied/rejected/blocked/not-applicable decisions
Detailed self-contained build contract packaged as references/build-contract.md, with a concise marker-managed root AGENTS.md block
Embedded Drupal CMS baseline for install, DDEV setup, Drupal CMS setup assistant or equivalent install path, content modeling, editor workflow, media, SEO, updates, and divergence tracking
Target-evidenced Drupal baseline guidance with the core Recipe runner, Recipe discovery before application, and continued Drush readback/config workflow

Embedded Drupal CMS best-practice defaults for goals-first content modeling, typed fields, taxonomy, Views planning, Media Library, SEO/social metadata, moderation, accessibility tooling, privacy/legal, email, backup/update workflow, and site settings
Drupal-native architecture guardrails for DDEV, managed media, Views, menus, aliases, editor displays, installed-substrate and Recipe-fit decisions, scoped gaps, and controller exceptions
Native-first Drupal API guidance: use Views, Canvas/Experience Builder, ECA, Entity/Field APIs, Configuration Management, roles/permissions, and maintained recipes before custom code
Node-vs-Canvas ownership guidance: nodes for reusable structured information, Canvas pages for one-off composed experiences, and hybrid node-data plus Canvas-presentation patterns where appropriate
Canvas authoring ownership gate: composed public routes must be the routes editors can maintain in Canvas/Experience Builder, with Utility Page exceptions and starter Canvas placeholders recorded

Composition modeling gate: flexible landing-like routes must declare their Drupal authoring owner, section ownership, editor mental model, expected editor actions, Canvas component model when used, and deviation records before implementation

Canvas component fidelity gate: public or rebuild-owned Canvas pages must use rational components, slots, typed props, Drupal-owned repeatable data, monolith detection, string-blob prop detection, and hardcoded Twig literal checks
Structured-content-first gate: recurring source objects must be modeled as Drupal content types, fields, taxonomy, media, relationships, and Views before import, theme polish, or Canvas composition
Custom-module last-resort guidance: prefer no custom module, then contrib/recipes/config/native primitives, and require reusable capability-shaped modules instead of source-site buckets

Optional companion skill recommendations: Drupal Canvas by default when skills are supported, stronger Drupal implementation companions, Drupal.org issue/contrib helpers, Acquia operations skills, and AGENTS.md as the conflict authority
Presentation-boundary guidance that keeps raw CSS, class names, HTML, JavaScript, and theme implementation out of editor fields
Route matrix, unfiltered Drupal readback, and field-to-output evidence gates from rebuild review feedback
Tool-neutral browser evidence gate for source/target visual comparison, visitor-facing behavior, and authenticated non-admin editor task proof

Next-cycle editorial gate for machine-backed temporal/taxonomy discovery, least-privilege future-cycle publish and anonymous-output proof, permission/workflow checks, zero-residue cleanup, and a live 404/410 recheck

Independent verification gate: a fresh verifier context must try to falsify completion claims against the live site, including per-route item counts, collection ownership, rendered embeds, target-required routes, route drift, Canvas placeholders, first-fold brand assets, editor add-a-row tasks, cold-reader labels, field-output behavior, off-road cleanup, and packet freshness

Blind adversarial review gate: a fresh reviewer that did not build the target must compare the original brief/source-of-truth materials to the target before reading builder rationale, with desktop/mobile route notes and raw evidence

Blind review verifier hardening: rejects null or non-object artifacts, statusless severe defects, mismatched severe-defect counts, missing primary-route coverage, missing screenshot evidence files, and fixed defects without resolving review passes
Completion authority split: authored review artifacts record observations, while only the final default live-target verifier derives completion authorization after live and packet checks

Packet completion-readiness checks reject untouched or blanket-filled templates, unresolved enum sentinels, open critical acceptance markers, generic claim evidence, stale source/target origins, reused or fake screenshots, weak editor evidence, and mismatched live Drupal identity or config state
Verifier consequence rule: packet-only validation, an unavailable or mismatched live target, or degraded required review leaves completion unauthorized; authored completion booleans cannot promote it
Durable intent hash convention: config_hash is sha256 of normalized exported config, solo agents can mark hash-valid, and accepted is reserved for human maintainer review

Collection ownership and item reconciliation gates: every list/grid/schedule/archive/gallery/search route must name its Drupal entity owner, View or collection owner, item counts, and editor add-a-row evidence
Raw embed and cleanup gates: raw iframe/script/source markup and direct database cleanup must feed off-road-inventory.md with safer Drupal-native or production-safe alternatives
Open decisions handoff: final handoff must present only human-owned decisions and keep agent-resolvable work in implementation gaps or active review loops

Blind-review route and evidence hardening: browser-first route expansion, Starter route cleanup, front-page alias decisions, config sync directory evidence, hardcoded public-string checks, and presentation/accessibility ownership checks
Evidence-ranked Drupal fundamentals from rebuild reviews: tracked config sync as source of truth, rendered SEO verification, non-admin editor-role checks, and off-road inventory review
Verifier-owned negative-route quality, access-wall canonical, rendered legal-link, and fresh per-route CDP before-consent network checks bound to the exact target and Drupal result state
Release-readiness tests for template naming, orphan templates, verifier argument handling, symlinked bin execution, structural packet success messaging, and npm-pack manifest drift
Pattern-map architecture guards for managed media, menu ownership, collection Views, custom controllers, marker modules, and raw rendering
Regulated-content governance guardrails for source/review status, disclosures, product/detail route QA, source-intent aliases, search, and SEO metadata

CONTRIBUTING.md
evidenceScope: This public package contains instructions, templates, gate vocabulary, a default live-target verifier, and a structural packet linter. Real runs produce their own source capture, target, QA, parity, and review evidence.
